title: Uplift
desc: Campus fitness app for Cornell's gym facilities.
body: Uplift provides Cornell students with a centralized platform to check gym hours, real-time capacity, and fitness class schedules across campus facilities. The app serves thousands of active users each semester and is maintained by a cross-functional team of engineers and PMs within Cornell AppDev. I joined as an Associate Product Manager, working across iOS and backend teams to prioritize features and ship iterative improvements.

highlight.label: Impact
highlight.text: Thousands of Cornell students rely on Uplift each semester to plan workouts — 400 Monthly Active Users.

meta.label: Team
meta.value: Team of 12 engineers, designers, marketers, and PMs

challenges: Aggregating real-time occupancy and scheduling data from inconsistent and unreliable gym data sources
Coordinating feature development across iOS and backend teams within a semester-based release cycle
Balancing new feature development with maintaining stability and meeting existing user expectations

title: CHEM
desc: Endowment management platform built for a nonprofit client through Cornell Hack4Impact.
body: CHEM is a full-stack web application developed for a nonprofit client through Cornell Hack4Impact. It streamlines the tracking of endowment funds, models allocation scenarios, and supports compliance reporting. The platform replaces a manual, spreadsheet-based workflow with a structured dashboard, making fund performance and regulatory requirements more transparent and accessible.

highlight.label: Client Impact
highlight.text: Replaced a manual spreadsheet workflow for a live nonprofit client — actively maintained and in production.

challenges: Modeling complex fund allocation logic aligned with the client's existing workflows
Designing a flexible data schema to accommodate evolving compliance requirements
Delivering a production-ready product within a fixed academic semester timeline

title: Portfolio
body: A hand-built static portfolio site developed without frameworks, using only HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript. The goal was full control over animations, interactions, and layout decisions. Features include a 3D carousel on the projects page, scroll-based reveal animations, a filterable icon grid, and a theme toggle with localStorage persistence.

highlight.label: Design Goal
highlight.text: Zero dependencies and no build step — fast, fully controlled, and easy to audit.

challenges: Building a smooth, physics-based 3D carousel with drag inertia using only Canvas and requestAnimationFrame
Designing a consistent visual system (typography, spacing, color tokens) from scratch
Implementing smooth scroll-based animations without layout performance issues

title: Timing
desc: Work completed during my software engineering internship at Timing.
body: Timing is a relationship management app. As a software engineering intern, I owned development of an LLM-powered relationship assistant that models user interaction history to generate contextual recommendations and proactive notifications. I also redesigned in-app contact onboarding flows and architected the cross-platform monorepo that underpins the product.

highlight.label: Highlight
highlight.text: Redesigned contact onboarding, reducing new contact creation time by 75% (60s → 15s).

challenges: Engineering prompt pipelines and feedback loops to improve LLM personalization accuracy over time
Architecting a scalable pnpm TypeScript monorepo (Next.js + Expo) with shared API layers for cross-platform feature parity
Implementing secure Supabase OAuth authentication with stable iOS simulator and dev-client builds

title: Sustainify
desc: Full-stack sustainability dashboard built at the Claude Builders Hackathon.
body: Sustainify is a full-stack web application built for the Claude Builders Hackathon. It enables users to track monthly and annual carbon emissions across categories, with interactive visualizations for trends and breakdowns. The project was built solo using Flask, React, and PostgreSQL, containerized with Docker.

highlight.label: Highlight
highlight.text: Built end-to-end at a hackathon — full Flask REST API, PostgreSQL backend, and interactive Chart.js dashboards.

challenges: Designing a data model flexible enough to capture emissions across diverse categories and time ranges
Building interactive, responsive Chart.js visualizations within hackathon time constraints
